title: acer barbatum f. rugelii
intro: Acer barbatum f. rugelii, also known as Rugel's maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. Acer barbatum f. rugelii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
